skill.name: competitor-profiling
skill.description: When the user wants to research, profile, or analyze competitors from their URLs. Also use when the user mentions 'competitor profile,' 'competitor research,' 'competitor analysis,' 'profile this competitor,' 'analyze competitor,' 'competitive intelligence,' 'competitor deep dive,' 'who are my competitors,' 'competitor landscape,' 'competitor dossier,' 'competitive audit,' or 'research these competitors.' Input is a list of competitor URLs. Output is structured competitor profile markdown files. For creating comparison/alternative pages from profiles, see competitors. For sales-specific battle cards, see sales-enablement.
skill.summary: Create structured competitor profiles from URLs with product, positioning, and pricing analysis.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 103 potential issues, all of which are false positives upon evaluation. The network findings are URLs in an evals test fixture, not executable code. The 'external_commands' findings are markdown code formatting (backticks) in documentation files, not actual shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false matches on marketing context words. This is a legitimate marketing research skill that guides an agent to analyze competitor websites and produce structured profile documents.

content.value_statement: Manually researching competitors takes hours of tab-switching. This skill turns a list of URLs into structured profile documents covering product, positioning, and pricing in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Generates structured competitor profile markdown files from input URLs
Analyzes product features, positioning, and target audience sections
Captures pricing models, packaging, and value metrics from competitor sites
Documents competitor strengths, weaknesses, and differentiators
Produces consistent profile templates for comparison and landscape mapping
limitations: Output quality depends on publicly available information on the provided URLs
Cannot access paywalled content, private dashboards, or authenticated pages
Pricing details may be outdated quickly and require manual verification
Does not generate comparison or alternative pages (use the competitors skill for that)

use_cases.title: Build a competitor landscape map
use_cases.description: Research 5-10 competitors and produce consistent profile files for a go-to-market strategy review.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a sales competitive deal
use_cases.description: Quickly profile a named competitor before a discovery call or competitive demo.

use_cases.title: Onboard a new hire to the market
use_cases.description: Generate a reading list of competitor profiles for a new product marketing or sales hire.

prompt_templates.title: Profile a single competitor
prompt_templates.prompt: Profile the following competitor using the competitor-profiling skill. Competitor URL: https://example.com

prompt_templates.title: Profile a list of competitors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the competitor-profiling skill to create profiles for each of these competitors: https://competitor1.com, https://competitor2.com, https://competitor3.com

prompt_templates.title: Profile a competitor with focus areas
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the competitor-profiling skill to profile https://example.com. Pay special attention to their enterprise pricing model, integration ecosystem, and any recent AI features they have announced.

prompt_templates.title: Profile a competitor for a battle card
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the competitor-profiling skill to profile https://example.com. Focus on sections that would feed into a sales battle card: positioning statements, key objections, and proof points we can counter.

output: Product: Cloud-based project management for mid-market teams
Positioning: 'Project management that scales with your team'
Target audience: Operations leaders at 50-500 person companies
Key strengths: Strong integrations, polished UI, established brand
Key weaknesses: Limited reporting, no native time tracking, higher price point

output_examples.input: Profile these competitors: https://alpha.com, https://beta.io, https://gamma.app
output: alpha.com: Analytics platform focused on e-commerce, positions on ease of use, $99-$499/mo tiers
beta.io: Developer-first observability tool, positions on OpenTelemetry-native, usage-based pricing
gamma.app: No-code internal tools builder, positions on speed to value, freemium to $30/seat/mo

best_practices: Provide 3-10 URLs at a time for consistent profile depth and quality
Include the competitor's homepage, pricing page, and a key feature page for the richest profiles
Review and verify pricing information manually, as website copy can lag current plans
Use the output profiles as inputs to the competitors skill for comparison-page generation
anti_patterns: Do not feed paywalled or login-only URLs and expect accurate profiles
Do not use this skill to generate comparison or alternative pages (use competitors instead)
Do not skip the verification step on pricing and feature claims before publishing externally

faq.question: What does the skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces structured competitor profile markdown files with product, positioning, pricing, and differentiator sections.

faq.question: How many competitors can I profile at once?
faq.answer: You can pass a single URL or a list of URLs. Smaller batches (3-10) tend to produce the most consistent results.

faq.question: Does this skill need API keys?
faq.answer: No. It uses the agent's ability to fetch and read web pages. No external API keys are required.

faq.question: How is this different from the competitors skill?
faq.answer: This skill creates individual profile documents. The competitors skill takes those profiles and builds comparison or alternative pages from them.

faq.question: Can it handle non-English competitor sites?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works with any publicly accessible web page regardless of language.

faq.question: Is the information always current?
faq.answer: No. Profiles reflect the page content at fetch time. Pricing and features change, so always verify before publishing.
